title: Data Binding in Vue.js Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of text interpolation, one-way attribute binding (v-bind), and two-way data binding (v-model).

questions.question: Which and/or what syntax is used for text interpolation in Vue templates?
Which directive and/or what shorthand is used to bind an HTML attribute (like src) to a dynamic value?
What is the primary role of two-way data binding (v-model)?
options: To only show data in the HTML.
To allow user input in HTML to automatically update the JS state and vice versa.
To prevent any data from ever reaching the server.

questions.question: Can you use JavaScript expressions inside double curly braces (e.g. {{ number + 1 }})?
options: Yes, as long as it's a single expression.
No, only property names are allowed.
Only in older versions of Vue.

questions.question: Is the 'Mustache' syntax ({{ }}) reactive in Vue.js?
options: Yes, it automatically updates whenever the underlying property changes.
No, it only renders once on page load.
